title: Samuel Jeffery Beside a Crackling Campfire in the Rural Wilderness of El Bolson Rio Negro
description: El Bolsón, Río Negro: Samuel Jeffery pauses beside a flickering nocturnal campfire, his hands cradling a traditional wooden mate gourd against the deep, encompassing shadows of the Andean wilderness. The ambient light from the flames casts a warm, golden glow across his features, creating a stark contrast against the impenetrable darkness of the surrounding forest vegetation. He sits in quiet contemplation on a weathered log, embodying the serene rhythm of Patagonian backcountry travel. The soft, diffuse glow highlights the rugged texture of his clothing and the rustic finish of the mate vessel, emphasizing a moment of stillness during an evening of outdoor immersion. Technically, the shallow depth of field isolates him from the indistinct backdrop, drawing focus to his steady, meditative presence as he prepares a infusion of yerba mate. This ritual, deeply ingrained in Argentine social fabric, serves as a bridge between the solitary exploration of the mountains and the communal spirit of local nomadic traditions. The play of light and shadow captures the essence of remote camping, where the simple act of preparing tea becomes an anchor for the traveler. This scene underscores the importance of slow, intentional engagement with the Patagonian landscape, highlighting how traditional habits foster a deeper connection to the wild geography of southern Argentina. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
